title: Pleioluma macrocarpa
intro: Pleioluma macrocarpa, commonly known as the 'Barringtonia' or 'Red Bead Tree', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Bioactive constituents of Pleioluma macrocarpa include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pleioluma macrocarpa is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and infections.
taxonomy: Pleioluma macrocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to New Caledonia. It is a member of the genus Pleioluma, which is a part of the Sapotaceae family. The Sapotaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sapote family. This family is part of the larger order Ericales, which includes a wide range of flowering plant families. The classification of Pleioluma macrocarpa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in its native ecosystem.
uses: I can find information on medicinal uses of Pleioluma macrocarpa.  Pleioluma macrocarpa, also known as the Queensland walnut tree, is used in traditional medicine by the indigenous communities in Australia to treat various health conditions.
